title: Petlomelia dipetala
taxonomy: Petlomelia dipetala is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. This family includes plants commonly known as dogbanes or oleander. Petlomelia dipetala is native to the tropical regions of the world and is often found in humid and subtropical climates. It is also known as the 'two-petaled petlomelia' due to its unique, two-petaled flowers. The classification of Petlomelia dipetala further includes its placement within the subfamily Rauvolfioideae.
uses: Petlomelia dipetala, also known as the tropical milk flower, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties.
